Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
 
 

________________________________

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com] 
Sent: Friday, March 07, 2008 7:48 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: Re: THE EMERGENT SPATIO-TEMPORAL BRAIN DYNAMICS OF LEARNING


Greetings,
 
I've reviewed the proposal sent to me by Jeffrey and am ready to discuss
it. Since I am on jury duty, if is impossible to reach me during the
day, but I would be pleased to talk in the evening or during the
weekend. I should have a better idea today, with any luck, how much
longer I will have to serve.

Best wishes,

EG

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate

in Clinical Neuropsychology

Jeffrey would like to hold the 3:30 meeting here at our office.  We are
located at 457 Madison Ave, 4th floor, between 50th & 51st.  We share a
courtyard with the NY Palace Hotel.  When the guys come to the
courtyard, they should NOT enter the hotel...they need to look left and
go in the door on the left (above the door it says "457")  They need to
tell the security guard they are here to see Jeffrey on the 4th floor
and he will let them up!

-----Original Message-----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Wed, 5 Mar 2008 8:49 am
Subject: RE: THE EMERGENT SPATIO-TEMPORAL BRAIN DYNAMICS OF LEARNING
I have forwarded on to Jeffrey!  Have a fabulous day and I will be in
touch soon regarding the 3:30 meeting place.
 
Lesley
 
From: egneurocog@aol.com <mailto:egneurocog@aol.com>
[mailto:egneurocog@aol.com <mailto:egneurocog@aol.com?> ] 
Sent: Tuesday, March 04, 2008 5:45 PM
To: Lesley Groff; Lesley Groff
Subject: THE EMERGENT SPATIO-TEMPORAL BRAIN DYNAMICS OF LEARNING
 
Dear Jeffrey,

Attached please find the research proposal, as promised. This is an
"ideal world" proposal, subject to modifications if necessary. Some of
the "fat" can be avoided if a way can be found to give direct grants to
participants. This would remove both the 30% fringe and the 10%
institutional overhead from a significant portion of the budget. In
principle, this is possible for the four principals' salaries. I
explored this route at some length, but was told that it may be
difficult for Lau and Thesen because of the nature of their visas. The
other components of the budget (post-doctoral fellows/graduate students
and experiment-related expenses) have to be channelled through the
university. 

I look forward to your feedback and will be glad to discuss this with
you further at your convenience.

Best wishes,
Nick

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
American Board of Professional Psychology
in Clinical Neuropsychology

315 West 57th Street
Suite 401
New York, NY 10019
USA

tel +1 212 541 6412
fax +1 212 765 7158
egneurocog@aol.com <mailto:egneurocog@aol.com> 
elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu <mailto:elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu> 
www.elkhonongoldberg.com <http://www.elkhonongoldberg.com/>

body_html:
From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com]
Sent: Tuesday, March 04, 2008 5:45 PM
To: Lesley Groff; Lesley Groff
Subject: THE EMERGENT SPATIO-TEMPORAL BRAIN DYNAMICS OF LEARNING

 

Dear Jeffrey,

Attached please find the research proposal, as promised. This is an "ideal world" proposal, subject to modifications if necessary. Some of the "fat" can be avoided if a way can be found to give direct grants to participants. This would remove both the 30% fringe and the 10% institutional overhead from a significant portion of the budget. In principle, this is possible for the four principals' salaries. I explored this route at some length, but was told that it may be difficult for Lau and Thesen because of the nature of their visas. The other components of the budget (post-doctoral fellows/graduate students and experiment-related expenses) have to be channelled through the university.

I look forward to your feedback and will be glad to discuss this with you further at your convenience.

Best wishes,

Nick

Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP

Clinical Professor of Neurology
New York University School of Medicine

Diplomate
